chunk_summary: Guidelines for preventing fortifier preparation errors in expressed and donor milk emphasize error-proofing, recipe standardization, hygiene, and time controls to ensure safety and nutritional accuracy.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of treating human milk fortifier (HMF) preparation as a high-risk process.
Learn proper hygiene and handling protocols for fortifier preparation to prevent contamination.
Recognize the role of recipe standardization and independent double-checks in reducing errors.
Apply time controls and storage guidelines to maintain milk safety and efficacy.
key_facts: Fortified milk should be refrigerated promptly and used within 24 hours.
Continuous feeding hang time is commonly limited to 4 hours per unit policy.
Powdered HMF is not sterile; minimize air exposure and prepare the smallest practical batch.
Use single-use sachets and avoid scooping from bulk containers to reduce contamination risk.
Label fortified milk with infant identifier, fortifier strength, and preparation time for accuracy.
reference_quote: Treat human milk fortifier (HMF) preparation as a high-risk medication process with error-proofing rather than relying on individual vigilance. (Reference 1)
